# ++CHRCH Access

*Section: THE BOOK OF INFLUENCE: Make Art Not Content*

- id: 20478410
- url: https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/20478410

## videos

- `iframe` https://dialog.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/
- `iframe` https://www.filestackapi.com/dialog/comm_iframe/
- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/controller-with-preconnect-5923ab14c9a2faa0ad2f0f95c16ea979.html#__shared_params__[version]=v3&__shared_params__[light_experiment_assignments]=%7B%22token%22%3A%22a97229ac-d1ff-410e-b6b4-a874cd068a41%22%2C%22assignments%22%3A%7B%22link_ewcs_prewarm_experiment_v2%22%3A%22control%22%7D%7D&apiKey=pk_live_hkgvgSBxG4TAl3zGlXiB1KUX&stripeJsId=a97229ac-d1ff-410e-b6b4-a874cd068a41&stripeObjId=sobj-6b1d9911-05bb-442d-a37c-33e1d53d9de3&firstStripeInstanceCreatedLatency=1435&controllerCount=1&isCheckout=false&stripeJsLoadTime=1778072671787&manualBrowserDeprecationRollout=false&mids[guid]=NA&mids[muid]=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&mids[sid]=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&referrer=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F20478410&controllerId=__privateStripeController8261
- `iframe` https://js.stripe.com/v3/m-outer-3437aaddcdf6922d623e172c2d6f9278.html#url=https%3A%2F%2Flearn.makeartnotcontent.com%2Fcourses%2F465756%2Flectures%2F20478410&title=4.%20Fuck%20%22Followers%22...%20Introducing%20%22Audience%20Mindset%22%20%7C%20The%20Mystery&referrer=&muid=53e126fd-04c3-48c6-890f-b04f0b26f45c352beb&sid=fdbd9602-a5fb-4729-889c-fc318f4a977cddf210&version=6&preview=false&__shared_params__[version]=v3

---

##    4\. Fuck "Followers"... Introducing "Audience Mindset" 

## If you're overwhelmed by social media you're doing it wrong.

## People are convinced that things have to be hard. Don't accept that narrative. Social media is supposed to be fun! And when things are fun, they become easy.

## Social media is like a musical instrument. You practice. You develop your style. And eventually you will be able to use the tools to play any tune you like. 

  


# **1\. ATTENTION ADDICTION**

First you should understand why people always want more followers.

They want attention.

They want validation.

And they see followers as a direct correlation to fulfilling that addiction.

Post some bullshit...

Then check your phone 140 times a day (That's an actual statistic. And it's sad.)

And that addiction leads to psychosis.

A warped view of reality.

You try to get followers on social media because it's easier than real life. It's the difference between NBA 2K20 and playing on a real court.

What would you have to do in the real world to get those same people to pay attention?

That would be a whole new ballgame, right?

That's why you're chicken shit of approaching strangers. The real stuff... (But we'll deal with that one soon, believe me...) 

So instead, you try to attract strangers by being clever online. Where your ego is safer.

I know, I know... Some people have succeeded at making this online "game" a full time job. Especially when it was a new thing back in 2015.

We gave them a name.

Influencers. 

But that's a lottery.

The Mystery School is not about creating influencers.

The Mystery School is about creating the people who influence the influencers. 

Artists.

Before you try to "grow" on Instagram... Let's do some housecleaning. 

From now on there will be less ego-stroking. And more "doing things with purpose". 

  


# **2\. AUDIENCE VS. FOLLOWERS**

If you stopped posting today. Who are the people who would notice? Who are the people who would miss you? 

The fans who like every post...

The ones leaving thoughtful comments...

The ones that, if you decide to sell something, they buy...

Who are the ones who would come to your "Instagram funeral"?

Those people are called your audience.

They are the only ones that matter.

And when you finally realize that... You've acquired the keys to your kingdom.

  


# **3\. NURTURE FIRST**

Before you try to attract new people to your audience you need to nurture the ones you already have.

You do that by getting to know them.

If you make a new friend, or you start dating someone, the first thing you do is try to get to know their likes and dislikes etc... And you look for that common ground. Right?

The digital world is no different. 

These are PEOPLE on the other end. Stop treating them like numbers.

Treat your Day 1s with care.

When you're too "big" to nurture your audience, you can hire fan clubs to do it for you... But for now... Do the "work".

Make it your duty to understand who your followers are. 

## Like my friend Dana who stopped her IG growth on purpose. She put her IG profile on private so she could focus on her audience and service them correctly... and then went HAM in the DMs.

That's the right energy.

  


# **4\. "REMOVE" followers**

Instagram shows you the least engaged followers you have and lets you silently remove them. It also shows you your "Least Interacted With".

Use those tools.

Unfollow people that don't align with the new you.

Start fresh to make your IG more succinct and representative of who you are becoming.

Oliver (mystery student) went in...

![](https://uploads.teachablecdn.com/attachments/j8kEH1qTmO5ZbGmShjcc_oliveryes2.jpg)

# **5\. DEFINE YOUR AUDIENCE**

Your audience is not everybody. It isn't the masses.

It's a community that corals around you for a reason.

What is that reason?

Once you know this... You can proudly take the next step.

Oh, and it's such a powerful move:

  


# **6\. PISS YOUR "FOLLOWERS" OFF...  **

Create a series of posts that are soooooo perfectly geared towards your core audience that everyone that doesn't fit in to your new IG's world view will quickly unfollow.

The double reaction you want is:

1\. some people saying "fuck this. unfollow." and...

2\. others will be like "Wow. I needed to see this today." 

That's how you know that you're on the right track.

As mystery student @msthighlicious who does this on a regular basis would say... 

"Good Riddance"

What if you concentrated all your energy into being so singular and hardcore about what you stand for that people were forced to either unfollow you or praise you for reading their mind?

Stop being afraid of pissing people off.

You're going to piss people off if you're doing anything decent. Accept this NOW.

It's ok to piss people off. It's good actually. It's engagement. Engagement that will lead to more people like the ones you helped already or that love your art... find you.

Haters are the perfect dog-whistle for attracting more of your true fans.

Piss your "followers" off... Gain an audience.

Remember the [Secondary Effects](https://learn.lastnightsparty.tv/courses/enroll/lectures/12084289) module?

If you go-in 100% on your message, maybe some people will leave... But give it time... your fans will find you.

## _Just like my friend Kate Noel who lost 5,000 followers after she started posting about her struggles with her weight. Before she did this house-cleaning, 80% of her followers were guys sending her dick pics. After house-cleaning, her audience was 60% women and a community of people interested in following her journey._

## **_Judge the power of your audience by how invested it is in your point of view. Not by how big it is._**

  


# **7\. PREPARE YOUR MIND FOR CONSISTENCY ("DROPPING BOMBS")**

Now that you're in the right "audience" frame of mind... there's one last step.

Prepare your mind for the upcoming work.

If you can't be consistent, you're not ready to grow an audience.

We can't all be @playboicarti . 

Only people that are consistent in the outside world don't need to be consistent on IG. But if IG is your main promotional hustle for getting your work known, then you'll need to start dropping bombs.

Dropping bombs doesn't mean only releasing controversial content.

It's just that what you post just needs to feel like "bombs" to your audience. It's whatever you've promised to deliver. It's the reason they came to you. It could be dope music. An insightful piece of writing. A resource that helps them.

But you need to give it to them on a schedule.

Don't try grow an audience that you can't service with that type of consistency.

That's why I'm mentioning it now. Get ready for it.

In 'The Book Of Influence' I'll be showing you how to make your online world a well-oiled machine. And then you can "scale".

But for now, prepare your mind for the relentless work of consistency.

  


# **8\. BETTER THAN HASHTAGS**

Imagine you're a t-shirt company with 500 followers and an audience of 100 that buys every time you release something? Uh... You have a business!

An audience that cares about you and that shares what you with others will make you grow faster than any clever or thirsty hashtag could ever do.

_“You don't need massive influencers to grow — you can truly do it if you have shareable content that people want to send to their friends.” Koreen (@werenotreallystrangers)_

  


[ Complete and Continue   ]()

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/20478410/comments/24577872)

I love the words 'audience mindset'. It has the implication of making you focus on who you're creating it for. Like, on the other side of the screen there's a living person with feelings. I used to just post stuff I liked with no particular focus on who was supposed to see it. Makes me think about how I could be inventive in trying new ways to promote or gather attention for my upcoming EP. Honestly, I've avoided posting for a long time since I was overthinking about what I should be posting or not. I'm dropping a single in a few weeks so good opportunity to try new things.

I've started doing this and it definitely makes a difference! I believe I give my audience the value of honest expression and self growth so I share books, music, and resources all the time through my weekly newsletter on Monday's and my discord. I'm at about 38 members right now but excited to see how big my audience grows by the end of the year.

Wow it’s been a year already! I’ve seen a major difference though. Stayed consistent with the newsletter, I’ve hit 255 ppl on my mailing list, and doing my daily practice. You gotta find ways to keep your audience ahead of everyone so they feel a part of the process.

I am a market researcher, spent my career looking at “followers” or customers segments and behavior to help grow brands and one of the most successful techniques is segmentation, identifying the golden customer segments of your brand and serve them - I didn’t think of this for my personal brand until this moment. Massive aha! Thank you!!

IRL ironically might be the way to learn and apply then to social... how to approach/apply for rappers/singers/producers though?

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/20478410/comments/16173127)

Need to engage in an exercise of interrogation... of who is my "audience" on socials, but also understanding what is present in that engagement and also what is missing. And then perhaps do the same with my IRL "audience". Since some of this has been happening naturally over the past year (and especially the 5 months since active listening of the podcasts (notes, tasks, journals, etc)... I think there is a gap to be identified that is exaggerated by algorithms. How is it "social" media when I can get more engagement from text messages and emails than I can from a social channel with 5-6k followers and can only reach 300 accounts with a post, right? C'mon, not even my OWN mom can see my stuff most of the time. Happy to quit it. But this lesson has me rethinking. I think I have some work in this process of reconciling the socials with IRL re: engagement (and impact) and would love any advice/direction you might offer on this. Even/especially if it means this falls into the category of overthinking. LOL

alaina

Awaiting Review 

4 years ago 

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/20478410/comments/15448822)

Wow. "Judge the power of your audience by how invested it is in your point of view..." I think that we could actually take a lot of pointers from you, FB. (Omg do you have another nickname that you'd prefer or do we just call you Father LOL.) When you specifically think about your audience, you've related to us so much so, that we don't even need to barely see what you look like. How anti social media is that??! And you've connected with us in such a deep way that we'd buy into anything that you'd speak for the most part because we are in total alignment with your point of view... because we hold the very same views. What I took from this lesson was the significance of capturing our audience at a heart level- which could never be fake or surfaced. To actually relate to those who we call our audience in an actual true way-and using you as an example of how it is that you relate to us. So much clarity here. Thanks.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)

The Mystery School is a macrocosm of everything that's being taught inside. You unlocked it. 👏 If you stay audience-obsessed, everything should unfold naturally for you as you build your YouTube.

I came back to this chapter looking for additional insight. I archived all my old posts (that were just saying hey look at me, I'm like you. Here is a shot from an airplane.) and began presenting/reading the writings I'm doing on my account. I hope to find an audience that cares about my take on things. I want to use any momentum to add to these readings with other techniques and build my writer/director audience.

[ __ Reply  ](javascript:void\(0\))[ __ Link  ](https://learn.makeartnotcontent.com/courses/465756/lectures/20478410/comments/14199457)

And get real people feedback. Talk to people in real life who are part of your audience and see what they listen to, read. There is no shortcut for that. It's what everyone avoids. Even brands I work with. Everyone thinks they KNOW. You don't KNOW until it's visceral.

![Ashris Choudhury](https://s.gravatar.com/avatar/4bc5e792ce4895a9603d9acf2dbb895e?d=mm)
